profile.name: Rob Weatherly
profile.title: Azure Architecture | Cloud Foundation Design | Lead Technical Engineering
summary: Azure-focused infrastructure architect and lead technical engineer with 25 years in IT and roughly 8 years focused on Azure architecture, implementation, migration, and operations.

Designs and builds Cloud Adoption Framework aligned Azure foundations for regulated and complex environments, with strengths in landing zones, hub-and-spoke networking, private connectivity, security governance, infrastructure as code, and operational handoff.

Known for being brought into ambiguous or difficult situations where the technical path, client trust, or delivery model needs to be reset. Strong client-facing operator who can defuse contentious conversations, clarify decision boundaries, challenge weak assumptions, and align engineering, security, operations, and executive stakeholders around a practical plan.

Uses AI as a force multiplier for solution design, research validation, documentation, and Bicep/IaC development while keeping final technical decisions grounded in evidence and tested implementation.

experience.title: Senior Consultant / Azure Infrastructure Architect
experience.summary: Azure architecture, cloud foundation implementation, regulated platform design, migration strategy, DevOps/IaC patterns, and client-facing technical leadership.
bullets: Architect and implement Cloud Adoption Framework aligned Azure foundations for regulated environments, translating business, security, identity, network, compliance, and operations requirements into landing-zone designs, Bicep modules, deployment runbooks, and operational handoff.

Build private-first Azure platform architectures across networking, hybrid connectivity, identity, encryption, observability, security posture management, and governance using landing-zone patterns and service-native controls for regulated workloads.

Lead complex Azure troubleshooting in under-documented environments, isolating failures across DNS, UDRs, firewall policy, private endpoints, Databricks connectivity, managed identities, Key Vault access, and environment isolation.

Create secure data-platform foundations across analytics, integration, storage, secrets management, and deployment layers, enabling data engineering teams through repeatable Dev/Test/Prod CI/CD patterns and operational handoff.

Design infrastructure deployment models with Azure DevOps, GitHub, ARM/Bicep, parameterized templates, variable groups, Key Vault overrides, and branch/promotion patterns that client teams can operate after handoff.

Turn regulatory and security expectations into practical controls, including Private Link adoption, least-privilege RBAC, managed identities, storage-key lockdown, diagnostic settings, encryption strategy, privileged-access review, and audit-ready evidence.

Assess and modernize Azure/infrastructure estates across landing-zone maturity, network segmentation, IAM, monitoring, backup/DR, cost hygiene, orphaned resources, private endpoint/DNS gaps, and migration readiness; developed Azure migration strategy for 2,000+ VMware workloads and 200+ application dispositions.

Reset troubled delivery situations by challenging unrealistic assumptions, reframing ambiguous scope into executable workstreams, building repeatable analysis tooling, and aligning technical and business stakeholders around defensible decisions.

Use AI-assisted engineering workflows to accelerate solution research, documentation, architecture validation, and IaC drafting while keeping final decisions grounded in source documentation, implementation tests, and client constraints.

experience.company: Earlier Infrastructure, Cloud, and Recovery Architecture Roles
experience.title: Infrastructure Cloud Architect / System Architect / Recovery Solutions Architect / Enterprise Architect / Technical Lead
experience.summary: Infrastructure, cloud, recovery, managed services, and enterprise architecture roles across automotive, healthcare/government data, disaster recovery, managed services, and outsourcing environments.
bullets: Managed Azure resources and architecture for business intelligence and data warehouse environments, including server, storage, backup, network, Hyper-V, Azure Government, and primary data center integration.
Led recovery architecture for managed disaster recovery customers, designing recoverable application environments aligned to RTO/RPO requirements and improving long-term business continuity posture.

Served as enterprise architect and technical lead for global managed services accounts, leading 30+ domestic and global technical resources, solutioning dozens of annual service requests, and generating roughly $1.5M per year in additional approved contract revenue on a major account.
Co-led SAP ECC migration from AIX/DB2 mainframe infrastructure to SLES Linux on VMware, with DR design using storage and server replication technologies to support 1-hour RPO and 5-hour RTO targets.

Helped turn around troubled delivery environments by improving issue resolution, maintenance processes, security/audit practices, and customer trust, including maintaining 9/10 customer satisfaction across multiple years on a major utility account.

engagementPortfolio.summary: Migration strategy for 2,000+ VMware workloads and 200+ application disposition patterns.

engagementPortfolio.summary: Azure-native application delivery and DevOps support across app hosting, identity, data, and AI-enabled application patterns.

aiPositioning: Uses AI-assisted engineering workflows to accelerate solution research, documentation, architecture validation, and IaC drafting.
Uses AI as a force multiplier, not as a substitute for tested implementation or source-backed decision-making.
Keeps final technical decisions grounded in source documentation, client constraints, and implementation evidence.
